expressions.english: a great success
an example of success
main factor of success
secret to success
to succeed
to be successful
to wish one success
What is your secret to success?
I finished it successfully.
expressions.notes: 끝내다 = to finish; to get + N + done

expressions.english: This time, the performance was successful.
I wish for the success of this concert.
expressions.notes: -(으)ㄹ게요. = I promise I will + V.; I will + V.

expressions.english: That person will definitely succeed.
expressions.notes:
분명히 = for sure, certainly, without doubt
-(으)ㄹ 것이다 = will; to be going to + V

expressions.english: This project was a great success.

expressions.korean: 운이 좋아서 성공한 사람은 별로 없어요.
expressions.english: There are not many people who succeeded because they were lucky.
expressions.notes:
운 = luck
운이 좋다 = to be lucky

expressions.korean: 성공 사례들을 잘 보면 공통점이 있어요.
expressions.english:
If you carefully look at some examples of success, there are common factors.
If you look hard at examples of success, there are things in common.
expressions.notes:
보다 = to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read
공통점 = things in common, common factors

expressions.korean: 성공하고 싶으면 그냥 열심히 하면 안 돼요.
expressions.english: If you want to succeed, you must not only work hard.
expressions.notes:
-고 싶다 = to want to + V
열심히 = (to do something) hard; diligently
-(으)면 안 되다 = shouldn't; must not

expressions.korean: 성공할지 실패할지는 해 보기 전에는 몰라요.
expressions.english: Whether you will succeed or fail, you will not know before you try it.
expressions.notes:
-(으)ㄹ지-(으)ㄹ지 = whether to + V.+ or to + V.
전 = before, ago

expressions.korean: 이 계획이 성공하지 못 해도 다음 계획이 있어요.
expressions.english: Even if this plan is not successful, I have a plan B.
expressions.notes:
-이/어/여도 = even though; no matter how much + S + V
다음 = next

expressions.korean: 이번 프로젝트는 성공할 가능성이 충분히 있어요.
expressions.english: This project has sufficient potential to succeed.
expressions.notes:
가능성 = possibility
충분하다 = to be enough, sufficient
충분히 is an adverb form of 충분하다.

expressions.korean: 그 영화는 흥행에는 성공했는데 나쁜 평을 많이 받았어요.
expressions.english: That movie had a successful run at the box office, but it received a lot of criticism.
expressions.notes:
나쁘다 = to be bad
나쁜 is the adjective form of 나쁘다.
평 = criticism; comment; review
